One-liner: Restaurants update their menu PDF anytime — the QR code never changes.
Cold Email Target: Restaurant owners / managers on local FB groups, Google Maps reviews
Experiment Type: Landing page + waitlist. Send cold email to 200 local restaurants with broken/missing menus.
Key Assumption to Test: Will restaurant owners pay €9/mo to avoid reprinting QR codes?
Agent Notes: Agent checks Google Maps for restaurants in a city, scrapes if they have a menu QR, cold-emails owners.

One-liner: Simple waiver signing for activity businesses. No DocuSign complexity.
Target Customer: Surf schools, climbing gyms, escape rooms, yoga studios
Cold Email Target: Activity business owners via Instagram DMs / local sport associations
Experiment Type: Landing page + 50 cold emails to local activity businesses. Offer free 30-day trial.
Key Assumption to Test: Do activity businesses struggle enough with paper waivers to pay for a digital solution?
Agent Notes: Agent scrapes Instagram for local activity businesses (surf, climbing, yoga tags) and DMs or emails.

One-liner: Paste a phone number + time, the service sends a reminder SMS. That's it.
Target Customer: Dog groomers, tutors, small clinics, hairdressers
Cold Email Target: Small service businesses — search Google Maps, Yellow Pages for grooming/tutor/salon
Experiment Type: Landing page. A/B test two value props: 'stop no-shows' vs 'look more professional'.
Key Assumption to Test: Will small service businesses pay €19/mo to reduce no-shows vs just texting manually?
Agent Notes: Agent sends cold emails to 500 local service businesses per city. Tracks open/reply rate.

One-liner: Issue branded PDF certificates for completions, courses, or memberships.
Target Customer: Online course creators, yoga studios, gyms, coaches
Cold Email Target: Online educators on Gumroad, Teachable, or Kajabi communities
Experiment Type: Landing page + cold email to 100 Gumroad/Teachable course creators.
Key Assumption to Test: Do course creators feel enough friction issuing certificates manually to pay €19/mo?
Agent Notes: Agent scrapes Gumroad/Teachable public courses, finds creator contacts, sends cold outreach.

One-liner: A simple 'join the waitlist' page small businesses share on Instagram.
Target Customer: Bakeries, pop-up shops, barbers, event vendors
Cold Email Target: Instagram small business owners — DM via agent on niche hashtags
Experiment Type: Landing page. Agent joins small biz Facebook groups and posts about it.
Key Assumption to Test: Will high-demand small businesses pay €9/mo for a waitlist tool vs a free Google Form?
Agent Notes: Agent monitors IG hashtags for 'soldout', 'waitlist', 'nextdrop' and DMs those accounts.

One-liner: After a job, send one SMS to the customer asking for a Google review.
Cold Email Target: Tradespeople and service businesses via Google Maps / local directories
Experiment Type: Landing page with ROI angle: '1 extra review/week = X new customers/year'.
Key Assumption to Test: Do tradespeople value Google reviews enough to pay €19/mo for automated asks?
Agent Notes: Agent scrapes Google Maps for businesses with <50 reviews and sends cold email.

One-liner: Manager builds the rota in a simple UI — staff get it by SMS. No app needed.
Target Customer: Cafés, small retail shops, restaurants, market stalls
Cold Email Target: Café / small shop owners on LinkedIn or local business associations
Experiment Type: Landing page + cold email to 100 small hospitality owners in a city.
Key Assumption to Test: Is WhatsApp rota sharing painful enough that owners pay €19/mo to replace it?
Agent Notes: Agent cold-emails hospitality businesses. Key objection to test: 'we just use WhatsApp'.

One-liner: Enter tips, staff count and hours — get a PDF breakdown for the accountant.
Cold Email Target: Restaurant managers and bar owners via hospitality Facebook groups
Experiment Type: Landing page + post in 5 hospitality FB groups.
Key Assumption to Test: Do venues calculate tip splits manually often enough to pay €9/mo to automate it?
Agent Notes: Low automation needed — mostly a content/community play. Agent posts in groups.

One-liner: Branded QR card for Airbnbs, hotels, cafés. Scan = password displayed nicely.
Cold Email Target: Airbnb hosts via Airbnb host communities on Facebook/Reddit
Experiment Type: Landing page + post in Airbnb host communities. One-time purchase option.
Key Assumption to Test: Will Airbnb hosts pay for this vs free QR generators? Test one-time vs subscription.
Agent Notes: One-time purchase model may convert better than subscription. Test both.

One-liner: Freelancers share a link with clients showing project status. No Jira needed.
Cold Email Target: Freelancers on Upwork, Fiverr, LinkedIn or Indie Hackers
Experiment Type: Landing page + cold email to top-rated freelancers on Upwork/Fiverr.
Key Assumption to Test: Do freelancers feel enough pain from 'what's the status?' client messages to pay €9/mo?
Agent Notes: Agent scrapes top Upwork/Fiverr profiles and emails them.

One-liner: One page to tell customers something changed — new hours, closed, moved.
Cold Email Target: Local businesses via Google Maps cold email
Experiment Type: Very low-friction landing page. Test one-time vs €5/month.
Key Assumption to Test: Is there enough pain to pay for this vs posting on Instagram/Facebook for free?
Agent Notes: Highest risk: free alternatives are good enough. Experiment cheap and fast.

One-liner: Put a sticker somewhere — change the URL behind it anytime.
Target Customer: Retailers, event organizers, landlords, museum displays
Experiment Type: Landing page + post on Product Hunt / Reddit.
Key Assumption to Test: Is the use case clear enough without explanation? Test copy variants.
Agent Notes: Needs strong copywriting to explain the 'sticker' use case quickly. Test 3 headlines.

One-liner: Stick on luggage/keys/pets. If found, scanner sees contact info without your number being public.
Cold Email Target: Travel communities on Reddit, pet owner Facebook groups
Experiment Type: Landing page + Reddit posts in r/travel, r/petadvice.
Key Assumption to Test: Will people pay €3/year when free alternatives exist? Test pricing sensitivity.
Agent Notes: Low revenue per user but very high volume potential. Consider freemium.

One-liner: Small businesses sell gift cards without a POS. Buyer pays, gets a code PDF.
Target Customer: Bakeries, hair salons, massage therapists, small restaurants
Cold Email Target: Small local business owners via Google Maps cold email
Experiment Type: Landing page + cold email to 200 local businesses before Christmas / Valentine's.
Key Assumption to Test: Will small businesses pay €19/mo + use it if setup is under 10 minutes?
Agent Notes: Timing matters — launch before gift-giving seasons. Stripe complexity is the main build risk.

Idea Name: Static File Hosting with Stable URL
One-liner: Upload a PDF/image, get a permanent URL that updates when you re-upload. No Dropbox needed.
Cold Email Target: Professional services (lawyers, clinics) via LinkedIn cold outreach
Experiment Type: Landing page. Test 'replace your Google Drive link' angle.
Key Assumption to Test: Is the 'stable URL' value prop clear without explanation to non-technical users?
Agent Notes: Very similar to I-01 (QR Menu). Consider bundling both into one product.

One-liner: Freelancers who just need 'book a 1h call with me' — no Calendly, no integrations.
Experiment Type: Landing page. Hard sell: Calendly free tier is a very strong competitor.
Key Assumption to Test: Can we win on simplicity + price vs Calendly's free tier? Very tough.
Agent Notes: High competition risk. Only experiment this if other ideas underperform.
